description: Figure 12 presents post-fracture EBSD characterization of the necking region in Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si low-density steel samples AN750-T, AN800-T, and AN850-T, processed via intercritical annealing at 750, 800, and 850 °C respectively followed by 400 °C bainitic aging. Panel (a) shows Kikuchi pattern quality overlaid with austenite phase maps (red), where retained austenite fractions decrease from 0.018 to 0.004 and 0.002 with increasing annealing temperature; yellow dashed boxes indicate magnified regions and yellow arrows mark characteristic line scan paths. Panel (b) displays corresponding kernel average misorientation (KAM) maps, highlighting hard regions (B/TM, FM) and ferrite (F), with red ellipses marking areas of high local misorientation. Panel (c) provides point-to-point (P-T-P) orientation difference profiles (red) and Kikuchi pattern quality values (black) along the characteristic lines crossing phase boundaries; sharp orientation jumps and pronounced minima in pattern quality occur at interfaces, accompanied by P-T-P_Ave values of 3.50°, 4.48°, and 3.50°. These observations demonstrate significant strain and geometrically necessary dislocation accumulation at soft-hard domain interfaces, confirming stress partitioning and strain-discontinuity accommodation in the multiphase heterogeneous microstructure after tensile deformation.
